_comment: Config for the email-sentry engine (run_sentry.py). The /email-sentry:init skill fills accounts, user_name, user_addresses, and timezone at setup. Tune vip_senders, quiet_hours, and claude.model. Everything here is read at runtime; no secrets live in this file.
_user_name_note: First name is enough. Injected into the judge prompt so 'a human wrote to you personally' has a name to check against.
_timezone_note: IANA name, e.g. America/New_York. Check with: timedatectl
_quiet_hours_note: Local to timezone above. During quiet hours the judge raises the bar to notify a PERSONAL account; a live WORK incident can still clear it. The judge enforces this from the injected CONTEXT.
_search_query_note: The engine builds the live query as: in:inbox is:unread <lookback> -label:<dedup_label>. Use --query on the CLI to override for testing (e.g. drop is:unread to judge already-read mail).
_accounts_note: One entry per gog-authenticated Gmail account. role is personal or work; role feeds the judge (work incidents clear the bar more easily, esp. in quiet hours).
_user_addresses_note: What counts as mail addressed 'to you': the account addresses plus any aliases, group addresses, or plus-addresses that land in these inboxes.

vip_senders._comment: STARTER LIST, tune it. An email from any of these biases the judge toward notify. Use lowercase. 'senders' = exact addresses; 'domains' = whole domains.

claude._comment: The LLM judge. model can be sonnet (default; balanced, good injection resistance) or haiku (cheaper, faster, less nuanced) or opus (most capable, priciest). The engine always adds --strict-mcp-config and --tools '' and runs from a neutral temp dir so no project/MCP context contaminates the judge.

notify._comment: LIVE mode only. The bot token is read at runtime from hotline's state .env (NOT stored here). chat_id null = auto: the first allowlisted chat in hotline's access.json. Set it explicitly to pin a recipient.
